mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate and borrowing costs, which raises the present value of future corporate cash flows and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day S&P 500 forward return in the lowest FEDFUNDS quartile does not significantly exceed the highest quartile (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals broad labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate wage income and consumer spending forecasts, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return during triggers is not significantly below the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production confirm a coordinated expansion in both final demand and manufacturing output, reducing recession probability and lifting revenue visibility, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances when both series rise jointly, indicating the interaction provides no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an above-trend jump in consumer prices forces the central bank to accelerate rate hikes, which increases the equity discount rate faster than nominal earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.3% while FEDFUNDS is above 1.0%
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered tightening-cycle episodes, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in industrial production signals recovering manufacturing capacity utilization and durable goods orders, which supports cyclical earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises more than 1.5% over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return during triggered periods is negative or not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 3% annualized confirms aggregate demand is running above potential, generating operating leverage for corporations and supporting positive earnings surprises that drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns during high-GDP-growth quarters do not significantly outperform low-growth quarters (Welch t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is elevated but nonfarm payrolls register a strong monthly gain, the joint signal indicates labor market healing through rising participation rather than discouraged-worker effects, boosting consumption forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 5.0% and month-over-month change in PAYEMS exceeds 200
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered joint-signal instances, or the interaction term adds no significant explanatory power in a bivariate regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in housing starts signals rising residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials, durable goods, and consumer credit, which lifts cyclical sector earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases more than 5% over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Rank correlation between 3-month HOUST change and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window.
